worlds.name: Number Adventure
worlds.subtitle: Tiny number missions that help you get stronger every day.

missionGroups.title: Make 5
missionGroups.subtitle: See tiny number friends that snap together to make 5.
missionGroups.parentPrompt: If she hesitates, cover one part and ask: what friend does this number need to make 5?
missionGroups.coachLine: Let’s spot tiny number friends that belong together.
missionGroups.celebration: You spotted number friends that make 5.

missionGroups.title: Make 10
missionGroups.subtitle: Build a full ten with friendly pairs you know.
missionGroups.parentPrompt: Prompt gently: what friend does this number need to make 10?
missionGroups.coachLine: Making 10 gives us a strong landing place for bigger sums.
missionGroups.celebration: Making 10 is starting to feel natural.

missionGroups.title: Count On
missionGroups.subtitle: Start at the bigger number, then hop forward.
missionGroups.parentPrompt: Encourage her to say the bigger number first, then tap or hop forward for the small add-on.
missionGroups.coachLine: We don’t need to count everything — we can start big and hop on.
missionGroups.celebration: You counted on from the bigger number like a quick mathematician.

missionGroups.title: Doubles
missionGroups.subtitle: Use easy twin facts.
missionGroups.parentPrompt: Prompt: do you know this double straight away?
missionGroups.coachLine: Doubles are fast facts we can remember.

missionGroups.title: Near Doubles
missionGroups.subtitle: Use a double, then one more or less.
missionGroups.parentPrompt: Prompt: what double is close to this one?
missionGroups.coachLine: A near double is just a double with a tiny change.
missionGroups.celebration: You used a clever near-double strategy.

missionGroups.title: Hop Through 10
missionGroups.subtitle: Make 10 first, then finish the sum.
missionGroups.parentPrompt: Prompt: what does the first number need to reach 10?
missionGroups.coachLine: Let’s hop through 10 because 10 is a friendly place.

missionGroups.title: Take Away
missionGroups.subtitle: Use little subtraction facts within 10.
missionGroups.parentPrompt: Prompt: start with the whole amount, then take some away.
missionGroups.coachLine: We start with the whole and take some away.
missionGroups.celebration: You are getting steadier with take-away facts.

missionGroups.title: Missing Part
missionGroups.subtitle: Find what number is hiding.
missionGroups.parentPrompt: Prompt: what goes with the first number to make the total?
missionGroups.coachLine: Sometimes subtraction is really a missing-part puzzle.
missionGroups.celebration: You found the missing parts like a number detective.

missionGroups.title: Back Through 10
missionGroups.subtitle: Step back to 10, then keep going.
missionGroups.parentPrompt: Prompt: can we step back to 10 first?
missionGroups.coachLine: Ten is a friendly stop when we jump backwards too.
missionGroups.celebration: You can jump back through 10 now.
